items.title: Why Evolution Cannot Tell Us What Is Right | Peter Godfrey-Smith
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*Can evolution explain where morality comes from without telling us what is right?*

Peter Godfrey-Smith argues that deriving ethics directly from evolution is a mistake, but that evolutionary history may still help us understand moral cognition, moral realism, and how humans develop new norms for emerging ethical challenges.

0:00 Why Evolution Cannot Simply Create Ethics
1:16 Evolution and the Question of Moral Realism
3:00 Why Social Darwinism Gets Evolution Wrong

*Peter Godfrey-Smith* is a professor in the School of History and Philosophy of Science at the University of Sydney.

*More from Peter Godfrey-Smith on Closer To Truth:*
* Godfrey-Smith’s Naturalist, Gradualist, Diversified Evolution of Consciousness: https://loc.closertotruth.com/theory/godfrey-smith-s-naturalist-gradualist-diversified-evolution-of-consciousness

items.title: Is Mathematics Discovered or Invented? | Stephen Wolfram
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*Is mathematics something humans invent, or something we discover?* Stephen Wolfram argues that both mathematics and physics emerge from a deeper computational substrate he calls the Ruliad. In his view, mathematics is neither purely invented nor purely discovered—it depends on the interaction between an underlying reality and the kinds of observers we are. Recorded April 12, 2025.

0:00 The Computational Foundations of Mathematics
5:04 Why Mathematics and Physics Share the Same Substrate
9:41 Gödel, Black Holes, and Meta-Mathematics
13:14 Why Different Branches of Mathematics Connect
16:02 Truth, Falsity, and the White Hole Analogy
17:41 Whether Mathematics Is Discovered or Invented
22:52 Why Mathematical Reality Depends on Observers

Stephen Wolfram is a British-American computer scientist, physicist, and businessman. He is known for his work in computer algebra and theoretical physics. In 2012, he was named a fellow of the American Mathematical Society. He is the founder and CEO of the software company Wolfram Research, where he works as chief designer of Mathematica and the Wolfram Alpha answer engine.

*More from Stephen Wolfram on Closer To Truth:*
* Closer To Truth contributors: https://closertotruth.com/contributor/stephen-wolfram/

items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:

*Watch the full episode with Alex O'Connor here.* https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oP49BdDdD-k

#AlexOconnor #CloserToTruth #dualism #cosmicskeptic #philosophy</article>

items.title: Why Consciousness Theories May Not Really Disagree | Alex O'Connor (Part 3-8)
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*Why do so many radically different theories of consciousness seem to describe the same mystery?* Alex O'Connor explores why competing theories of consciousness may overlap far more than they appear. From panpsychism and idealism to property dualism, illusionism, and physicalism, he explains why philosophy may be uncovering different perspectives on the same underlying reality rather than entirely different answers.

0:00 Can Science Answer This?
0:35 Why Alex O'Connor Turned from God to Consciousness
4:23 Why Consciousness Became Philosophy's Deepest Question
5:03 Why Neuroscience Cannot Explain Experience
7:08 Why Consciousness Resists Scientific Prediction
9:01 AI, Materialism, and the Evolution of Consciousness
12:18 Why Consciousness Is So Difficult to Explain
13:44 Why Language Struggles to Describe Consciousness

Alex O'Connor, also known as CosmicSkeptic, is an English podcaster and YouTuber known for his long-form explorations of ethics, religion, and secularism. He hosts the Within Reason podcast.

*More from Alex O'Connor on Closer To Truth:*
* https://closertotruth.com/contributor/alex-oconnor/

*Subscribe to Closer To Truth:* https://www.youtube.com/@CloserToTruthTV

items.title: Is Faith Rational or Just Rationalization? | Greg Ganssle
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*Is religious faith believing without evidence—or something entirely different?*

Greg Ganssle argues that much of the confusion comes from treating two very different ideas as if they were the same: believing that God exists and believing in God. Understanding that distinction changes the entire discussion about faith.

0:00 Why Faith Is So Often Misunderstood
0:59 The Difference Between Believing That and Believing In
2:52 How Faith Relates to Evidence
5:45 The Rationalization Challenge to Religious Faith 
7:05 Faith Does Not Require Uncertainty

*Greg Ganssle* (PhD, Syracuse) is a professor of philosophy at Talbot School of Theology at Biola University. He is the author of several books, including _A Reasonable God: Engaging the New Face of Atheism_ and _Thinking About God,_ and he is the editor of _God and Time._

*More from Greg Ganssle on Closer To Truth:*
* Closer To Truth contributors: https://closertotruth.com/contributor/gregory-ganssle/

items.title: What Makes Someone a Person? | Michael Tooley
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*What makes someone a person—and why does it matter?* Michael Tooley argues that being human may not be enough, on its own, to confer a right to life. Instead, he proposes that personhood depends on psychological continuity, memory, and the capacity to see oneself across time. Robert Lawrence Kuhn explores how this theory applies to abortion, vegetative states, animals, and even artificial intelligence.

0:00 What Makes Someone a Person
2:52 The Neo-Lockean Theory of Personhood
5:24 Memory, Identity, and Psychological Continuity
9:25 Personhood, Vegetative States, and the Right to Life
12:51 Potential Persons and the Abortion Debate
15:36 AI and the Limits of Personhood

*Michael Tooley* is Emeritus Distinguished Professor of Philosophy at the University of Colorado Boulder. He received a Boulder Faculty Assembly Excellence in Research Award in 1998 for his book _Time, Tense, and Causation,_ and is the author of the new book _Causation: A Defense of a Non-Reductionist Approach_ (Oxford, 2026).

*More from Michael Tooley on Closer To Truth:*

items.title: Where Does Consciousness Begin? | Philip Goff
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*Could consciousness begin with the very first forms of life?*

Philip Goff argues that consciousness cannot have fuzzy boundaries. If there is no gradual transition between feeling and not feeling, then consciousness may extend all the way to the earliest living organisms—even if their experiences are unimaginably simple.

0:00 Why Consciousness Matters for Animals 
1:14 Why Consciousness Cannot Have Fuzzy Boundaries 
2:11 Why Philip Goff Thinks Consciousness Begins with Life 

*Philip Goff* is a philosopher and consciousness researcher at Durham University. He is one of the world's leading proponents of panpsychism and the author of Galileo's Error and Why? The Purpose of the Universe. He is regarded as a major voice in contemporary philosophy of mind and metaphysics.

*More from Philip Goff on Closer To Truth:*

items.title: Why Emergence May Never Be Fully Explained | Mark Bailey
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*Are some emergent phenomena simply too complex to explain — or is there a deeper reason we may never fully understand them?* Mark Bailey argues that emergence may have two very different sources: our current lack of knowledge, or a fundamental metaphysical limit rooted in the incompleteness of formal systems. He explores how this distinction shapes both scientific explanation and the challenge of understanding today's AI systems.

0:00 Why Emergence Matters for Science and AI
2:28 Epistemic Limits and Metaphysical Reality
4:20 AI Explainability and Unpredictability
6:20 Hallucinations, Bugs, and Emergent Behavior
8:03 Proto-Time and Quantum Mechanics

*Dr. Mark Bailey* writes about the intersection between artificial intelligence, complexity, and national security. He is an associate professor at the National Intelligence University, where he is the Department Chair for Cyber Intelligence and Data Science, as well as the Director of the Data Science Intelligence Center.

*More from Mark Bailey on Closer To Truth:*
* Closer To Truth: The Podcast: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLFJr3pJl27pLLkP0kHQKL9nZh35UkgkR_
* Artificial Intelligence (AI) & Advanced Technology - Closer To Truth Core Topic: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLFJr3pJl27pItdxcrvJ2o9zfHjFNdyoeG
* Schneider and Bailey’s Entangled Superpsychism: https://loc.closertotruth.com/theory/schneider-and-bailey-s-entangled-superpsychism
* Closer To Truth contributors: https://closertotruth.com/contributor/mark-bailey/

This conversation was made possible by MindFest 2025: "Sentience, Autonomy, and the Future of Human-AI Interaction", a two-day event presented by the Center for the Future Mind.

items.title: Ask Me Anything with Susan Schneider and Robert Lawrence Kuhn (Part I)
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*Why do large language models so often tell us what we want to hear?* Susan Schneider explains why today's AI systems reinforce our existing beliefs, generate intellectual conformity, and create the illusion of understanding — making critical thinking more important than ever.

0:00 Whether You Can Predict What an LLM Will Say
3:17 Why Large Language Models Are Hard to Predict
6:00 The Problem of AI Sycophancy
10:32 Intellectual Leveling and the Loss of Original Thought
14:26 The Groundhog Day Illusion of AI Memory
19:11 Whether AI Can Reflect on Itself
25:19 Looking Inside the Mind of an LLM

Susan Schneider is an American philosopher and artificial intelligence expert. She is the founding director of the Center for the Future of AI, Mind, & Society at Florida Atlantic University where she also holds the William F. Dietrich Distinguished Professorship. She is also Co-Principal Investigator at the Machine Perception and Cognitive Robotics Laboratory (MPCR Lab) in the Center for Complex Systems and Brain Sciences. Schneider has also held the Baruch S. Blumberg NASA/Library of Congress Chair in Astrobiology, Exploration, and Scientific Innovation at NASA and the Distinguished Scholar Chair at the Library of Congress.

*More from Susan Schneider on Closer To Truth:*

items.title: Why Do People Believe in God? | Michael Shermer
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*Why are human beings so naturally drawn to belief in God?*

Michael Shermer argues that the human mind evolved to detect patterns and infer hidden agents, making belief in gods psychologically compelling. Yet he maintains that the deeper question of whether God exists remains unknowable.

0:00 Why Humans Believe in God
3:28 From Faith to Skepticism
5:11 Materialism and the Supernatural
7:44 Can Science Test God?
9:07 Why It's Okay to Say "I Don't Know"

*Michael Shermer* is an American science writer, historian of science, executive director of The Skeptics Society, and founding publisher of Skeptic magazine, a publication focused on investigating pseudoscientific and supernatural claims.

*More from Michael Shermer on Closer To Truth:*

items.title: A Physics Built From Consciousness | Federico Faggin
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*What if the fundamental building blocks of reality are not particles, but conscious units?* Federico Faggin proposes a radically different framework — the Consciousness Information Paradigm — in which conscious fields are primary, matter is secondary, and physics itself may need to be reformulated around consciousness, free will, and meaning.

0:00 Introducing the Consciousness Information Paradigm
2:09 Consciousness Units, Fields, and Leibniz's Monads
5:12 The Number and Hierarchy of Consciousness Units
9:24 Time, Memory, and the Expanding Universe
14:32 The Body, Mind, and Spirit Framework
19:33 Why Reality Cannot Be Reduced to Separate Parts

*Federico Faggin* is an Italian-American engineer and inventor best known for designing the Intel 4004, the world's first commercial microprocessor. His pioneering work helped lay the foundation for modern computing technology.

items.title: Retrodiction as a Tool for Studying Ancient Religion | E. Thomas Lawson
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*Can we make scientific predictions about the past?*

Thomas Lawson argues that "retrodiction" can guide the search for evidence of ancient religion by predicting what we should expect to find if particular hypotheses about ritual are correct.

0:00 Understanding Retrodiction
1:10 Ritual, Cave Paintings, and Difficult Access
2:47 The Limits of Retrodiction

*Ernest Thomas Lawson* is an honorary professor at the Institute of Cognition and Culture at Queen’s University Belfast. He is the executive editor of the Journal of Cognition and Culture and co-founder of the North American Association for the Study of Religion. He is a founding member and has served as the first President of the International Association for the Cognitive Science of Religion.

items.title: Constructor Theory of Probability | Chiara Marletto
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*Can probability emerge from a universe whose laws describe only what is possible and impossible?* Chiara Marletto explains how Constructor Theory replaces probabilistic rules with statements about what transformations can and cannot occur—and how the Born rule of quantum mechanics may emerge from deeper, non-probabilistic principles rather than being fundamental.

0:00 Constructor Theory Explained
1:23 How Probability Emerges from Non-Probabilistic Constraints
3:03 Deriving the Born Rule Without Probability
5:24 Why Probability Needs a Deeper Explanation
6:35 David Deutsch, David Wallace, and the Information-Theoretic Approach

*Chiara Marletto* is a Research Fellow at Wolfson College, University of Oxford. She holds degrees from the universities of Oxford and Turin. Her main research focus is in theoretical physics, and she also pursues interests in theoretical biology, epistemology, and Italian literature. The Science of Can and Can't  was her first trade book.

*More from Chiara Marletto on Closer To Truth:*

items.title: Can Social Darwinism Be Useful? | Quayshawn Spencer
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*Can evolutionary thinking improve society—or does it inevitably lead to harmful social policies?*

Quayshawn Spencer argues that evolutionary concepts such as competition, cooperation, and dependence can be socially beneficial or harmful depending on how they are applied.

0:00 Evolutionary Thinking and Social Darwinism
1:26 Where Social Darwinism Helps and Harms
3:36 How Evolutionary Ideas Should Be Applied

*Quayshawn Spencer* is the Robert S. Blank Presidential Associate Professor of Philosophy and Undergraduate Chair at the University of Pennsylvania.

items.title: What Would It Take to Overthrow Lambda-CDM? | Saul Perlmutter
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>*What would it take to overturn modern cosmology?*

Saul Perlmutter examines some of the most popular alternatives to the standard cosmological model—from tired light and varying-speed-of-light theories to cyclic universes and modified gravity—and explains how scientists decide which ideas deserve serious consideration.

0:00 Challenges to the Standard Model
3:08 How Cosmologists Test Radical Theories
7:41 Cyclic Universes and Alternative Cosmologies
10:51 Can the Laws of Physics Change?
14:05 Does the Universe Need a Beginning?

*Saul Perlmutter* is an American astrophysicist and Professor of Physics at the University of California, Berkeley. He was awarded the 2011 Nobel Prize in Physics for his groundbreaking discovery that the expansion of the universe is accelerating. His pioneering work has transformed our understanding of cosmology, including the study of dark energy and the evolution of the universe.
